title: Podman storage gets corrupted when pulling an image
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to get an image to run with Podman, defined as a quadlet file.
`systemctl --user start authentiek-server.service` fails with exit code 125:

```
Sep 10 09:49:52 host authentik-server[711811]: time="2025-09-10T09:49:52+02:00" level=error msg="Image ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4 exists in local storage but may be corrupted (remove the image to resolve the issue): loading primary layer store data: error removing stale temp dir /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/tempdirs/temp-dir-1618383482: unlinkat /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/tempdirs/temp-dir-1618383482/1-c7ce558278b619ac541fc61a1267e58df72ccf8e624c87002b313a8a65f17207: directory not empty"
Sep 10 09:49:52 host authentik-server[711811]: Trying to pull ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4...
Sep 10 09:49:52 host authentik-server[711811]: Pulling image //ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4 inside systemd: setting pull timeout to 5m0s
Sep 10 09:49:56 host authentik-server[711811]: Getting image source signatures
Sep 10 09:49:56 host authentik-server[711811]: Copying blob sha256:15b2a750b48ae57cf27d2f075e6375860496c115f0e61bed5c7b97d3a1d54c0e
...
Sep 10 09:49:56 host authentik-server[711811]: Copying blob sha256:648fcb0e95e6aacd293cb96fc78f7feedd197e41e792f52bac248272ef46b0ce
Sep 10 09:49:56 host authentik-server[711811]: Error: unable to copy from source docker://ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4: copying system image from manifest list: trying to reuse blob sha256:61320b01ae5e0798393ef25f2dc72faf43703e60ba089b07d7170acbabbf8f62 at destination: looking for layers with digest "sha256:61320b01ae5e0798393ef25f2dc72faf43703e60ba089b07d7170acbabbf8f62": loading primary layer store data: error removing stale temp dir /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/tempdirs/temp-dir-1618383482: unlinkat /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/tempdirs/temp-dir-1618383482/1-c7ce558278b619ac541fc61a1267e58df72ccf8e624c87002b313a8a65f17207: directory not empty
Sep 10 09:49:56 host podman[711811]: 2025-09-10 09:49:56.132091414 +0200 CEST m=+3.537743714 image pull-error  ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4 unable to copy from source docker://ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4: copying system image from manifest list: trying to reuse blob sha256:61320b01ae5e0798393ef25f2dc72faf43703e60ba089b07d7170acbabbf8f62 at destination: looking for layers with digest "sha256:61320b01ae5e0798393ef25f2dc72faf43703e60ba089b07d7170acbabbf8f62": loading primary layer store data: error removing stale temp dir /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/tempdirs/temp-dir-1618383482: unlinkat /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/tempdirs/temp-dir-1618383482/1-c7ce558278b619ac541fc61a1267e58df72ccf8e624c87002b313a8a65f17207: directory not empty
Sep 10 09:49:56 host systemd[1170]: authentik-server.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
```

I don't think is an issue with that image in particular, I've tried a more recent one (ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.8.1) and I get the same issue if I try to run `ghcr.io/haveagitgat/tdarr:latest`.

The issue appears only when I execute the command `systemctl --user start authentiek-server.service`. If I run the command `podman pull ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4` I don't get any issue but the container fails to start with the systemctl command anyway:

```
podman@host:~> podman pull ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4
Trying to pull ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 15b2a750b48a skipped: already exists
...
Copying config 96abf2164c done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
96abf2164c7947bdbb3a90ca2087322fdf2979e8a29b129ada3aae08a8e9cfba
```

I've already tried to delete the image and pulling again, same result.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Try to run ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4 or ghcr.io/haveagitgat/tdarr:latest
2. Issue pulling the image when starting the container with systemctl --user command


### Describe the results you received

```
podman@host:~> systemctl --user restart authentik-server.service
Job for authentik-server.service failed because a timeout was exceeded.
See "systemctl --user status authentik-server.service" and "journalctl --user -xeu authentik-server.service" for details.
```

### Describe the results you expected

To have a running container from the given image

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
would be good to know what `authentiek-server.service` does and how it is defined.

Do you have any SELinux denial message in the audit log?

Thank you @giuseppe for your quick reply.
Regarding the SELinux message, I see nothing related (but interesting to see messages like this...):

```
host~ # ausearch -m avc --start 09/09/2025
----
time->Tue Sep  9 23:08:15 2025
type=AVC msg=audit(1757452095.913:126): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=9326 comm="chown" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=48316 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c649,c786 tcontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
----
time->Tue Sep  9 23:08:15 2025
type=AVC msg=audit(1757452095.913:127): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=9326 comm="chown" name="" dev="pipefs" ino=48317 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c649,c786 tcontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tclass=fifo_file permissive=0
```

This is the quadlet definition:

```
[Unit]
Description=Authenitk
After=local-fs.target custom-app-network.service

[Container]
Image=ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:2025.4.4
ContainerName=authentik-server
HostName=authentik-server.internal
AutoUpdate=registry
Environment=AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__HOST=host.containers.internal:5432
Environment=AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__NAME=authentik
Environment=AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__PASSWORD=password
Environment=AUTHENTIK_POSTGRESQL__USER=authentik
Environment=AUTHENTIK_REDIS__HOST=host.containers.internal:6379
Environment=AUTHENTIK_SECRET_KEY=5QK7auACOH1h6adEzmR7PErDp0MsdobipPAYGCNTN6lH8E3M9Wc5g8rb+xwJf1ccP9CUfwndn9CvYZFO
Network=custom-app.network
PublishPort=9000:9000
PublishPort=9443:9443
User=0
UserNS=keep-id
Volume=${CONFIG_DIR}/authentik/media:/media:Z
Volume=${CONFIG_DIR}/authentik/custom-templates:/templates:Z

# Health Checks
HealthStartPeriod=5m
HealthCmd=CMD-SHELL curl -f --insecure http://localhost:8080 || exit 1
HealthInterval=30s
HealthRetries=2
HealthOnFailure=kill

[Service]
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

would it be possible to try again disabling temporarily selinux (`setenforce 0`)?

It might be a `dontaudit` rule, and to see it in the logs, you first need to run `semodule -DB`
I ran `setenforce 0` and then `systemctl --user restart authentik-server`, I get the same issue.
@giuseppe I just tried the same quadlet file in rootful, it works without any problem.
Can you run `podman system check` and maybe with the `--repair` option to see if it fixes the storage.

Also to ask the obvious what is in /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/tempdirs/temp-dir-1618383482/1-c7ce558278b619ac541fc61a1267e58df72ccf8e624c87002b313a8a65f17207? Can you remove the dir yourselves.

AFAIK the directory not empty error should only happen if there are still files in it but in we use the golang function RemoveAll() which should delete it recursively. So either something adds file to it while it tries to get deletes (unlikely) or there is some other error condition for ENOTEMPTY that I am unaware of.

@Luap99 inside that directory, there was an empty directory called `merged`. I removed it and restarted the container, same issue.

I think the issue might be else where though: as a test I disabled all quadlets and rebooted the system.
I then deleted directory `~/.local/share/containers/storage`. 
I started a single container (postgres pgvector v16) and run `podman system check`, The latter already returns error like this:

```
Damaged layer c859be561ea8e463cec133c916779079a09f601334cfdeabd8c6396863ef238a:
138 errors occurred:
	* layer c859be561ea8e463cec133c916779079a09f601334cfdeabd8c6396863ef238a: /etc(mtime:0x68ba35c1￫0x68c151ce), layer content modified
	* layer c859be561ea8e463cec133c916779079a09f601334cfdeabd8c6396863ef238a: /etc/alternatives(mtime:0x689e2890￫0x68c151cd), layer content modified
	* layer c859be561ea8e463cec133c916779079a09f601334cfdeabd8c6396863ef238a: /etc/dpkg(mtime:0x68ba3599￫0x68c151cd), layer content modified
```

That container running, doesn't seem to have any issue though.

it could be that podman inside the systemd service gets a different run dir so that it doesn't see the pause process that is already running and creates a new user+mount namespace and it messes up with the mounts.

Do you have any custom configuration for systemd to harden services?  Anything like private mounts?

I'm not sure if I have any custom configuration for systemd to be honest.
is there any command I can run so I can provide the output?

How large is the image? As rootless UserNS=keep-id has to copy + chown the entire image so that can take a while and maybe you run itno the systemd start timeout and then podman was killed in a bad state?

That would match the error message systemd gives you.
> failed because a timeout was exceeded.

Try setting a high TimeoutStartSec= value under the [Service] section.

Hi both,
thank you so much for your quick replies and help!

@Luap99 I think that was the issue. Being the image fairly large (1.31GB) I think I was hitting a timeout. Adding `TimeoutStartSec=9000` to the `[Service]` section, did the trick.
User mapped, no timeout issues.

Thanks again!
